articles.title: Forget Bengal, we will dethrone BJP from Delhi: CM Mamata Banerjee after phase-1 turnout
articles.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee declared the high phase-one voter turnout a decisive mandate against the BJP, predicting the saffron party would lose significantly. She accused the BJP of atrocities and warned of "democratic revenge" for their actions, urging focus on Manipur's unrest and criticizing their treatment of allies like Nitish Kumar.

articles.title: 'One little half sentence' & ridiculous SC ruling on tariffs could have saved US $159 billion, says Trump
articles.description: Former President Donald Trump has strongly criticized a recent United States Supreme Court decision regarding tariffs. He stated the ruling could lead to the country repaying companies approximately $159 billion. Trump believes a minor alteration in the court's wording could have prevented this significant financial outflow. This development impacts government revenue and trade relations.

articles.title: PCMC cracks down on errant STPs; ₹70,000 fine imposed for releasing untreated sewage
articles.description: PCMC penalises 12 housing societies for violating environmental norms by discharging untreated or partially treated sewage into nallahs and rivers

articles.title: Haridwar: Man rescued from 40-foot-deep borewell after 10-hour op
articles.description: A man was rescued from a 40-foot-deep borewell in Haridwar after a 10-hour joint operation by SDRF, NDRF, police and fire service teams. The victim, Manohar, met with an accident while working at the borewell in Kangri village.

articles.title: Paige Shiver Michigan: Everything We Know About the Relationship That Got Sherrone Moore Fired
articles.description: Paige Shiver, the former Michigan football staffer at the center of Sherrone Moore's firing, has spoken publicly for the first time, saying their relationship was an "open secret" within the athletic department and that university leadership failed to protect her because they were more focused on winning football games than addressing Moore's behavior.

articles.title: Senior Kerala journalist accused of sexual harassment in train, arrested

articles.title: Virat Kohli becomes third batter after Chris Gayle and Rohit Sharma to ...
articles.description: Virat Kohli has achieved another IPL milestone, surpassing 300 sixes in the league. He now joins the elite club of Chris Gayle and Rohit Sharma, becoming the third player to reach this mark. Kohli's remarkable consistency continues to etch his name in IPL history, further solidifying his legendary status.

articles.title: Is your toddler constantly hitting you and others? Common triggers and how to control this behaviour
articles.description: Toddlers often hit due to frustration, overstimulation, or unmet needs. Parents can manage this behavior by staying calm and redirecting their child. Teaching toddlers to express emotions and demonstrating gentle touch are key. Understanding these triggers helps parents respond effectively. This approach fosters better emotional regulation in young children.

articles.title: Economist Ashok Lahiri from Bengal to become Niti Aayog vice-chairman, Gobardhan Das named as member
articles.description: Economist Ashok Lahiri and scientist Gobardhan Das from West Bengal are set to join Niti Aayog. Lahiri, a former chief economic advisor, will be the vice-chairman. Das, director of IISER Bhopal, will serve as a member. This appointment brings distinguished expertise to the policy think tank. Their contributions are expected to shape future economic and scientific initiatives.

articles.title: Ashok Lahiri to be NITI Aayog vice-chairman, Gobardhan Das member: Report
articles.description: Lahiri is a former chief economic advisor to the government and has served as a member of the 15th Finance Commission

articles.title: Health data of 500,000 UK volunteers listed for sale on Chinese platform, alarm raised
articles.description: Sensitive UK Biobank health data, potentially affecting nearly 500,000 participants, was briefly listed for sale on a Chinese marketplace. British authorities acted swiftly to remove the listings, but the incident has raised fresh concerns about the security of the vast research database.

articles.title: Himalayan snow cover down 27.8%, lowest in 2 decades: Report
articles.description: Experts warn of lower summer runoff, rising water stress risks

articles.title: 'Answer the question!': Senators torch Soros-funded witness at fiery Minnesota Somali fraud hearing
articles.description: Heated moment unfolded at the Senate Judiciary Committee as Sen. Ted Cruz accused top Minnesota Democrats of being aware of — and allowing — large-scale fraud schemes tied to Somali-linked nonprofit networks. Cruz directly named state leaders, claiming warning signs were ignored while millions in taxpayer funds were allegedly misused. The hearing quickly turned confrontational, with Cruz clashing sharply with a witness over accountability and oversight failures.

articles.title: NGT flags unstable hanging glaciers in Himalayas
articles.description: Dehradun: National Green Tribunal (NGT) has taken suo motu cognisance of media reports highlighting environmental risks posed by unstable hanging glac.

articles.title: US Navy faces huge outrage, Iran fumes: This could be the most shocking Naval incident of the year?
articles.description: US Navy Faces Huge Outrage, Iran Fumes: This could be the most shocking naval incident of the year. In this video, we unpack the clash at sea that has set Washington on the defensive and sent Tehran into a fresh wave of anger. From the sinking of an Iranian warship by a U.S. submarine to escalating encounters across the wider region, we break down what actually happened, why Iran is calling it an “atrocity,” and why legal and diplomatic questions are now being raised about American actions at sea. Is this a legitimate act of war, a preemptive strike, or a dangerous overreach that could spiral into something far bigger?

articles.title: India launches indigenous silicon photonics tech at IIT Madras in semiconductor self-reliance push
articles.description: MeitY said the IIT Madras-developed silicon photonics solutions will help startups, industry, academia and defence design advanced photonic chips in India.

articles.title: REPLAY: Press briefing by US Defense Secretary
articles.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Operation Epic Fury has achieved a decisive result in just weeks, focused on preventing Iran from developing nuclear weapons. He noted the mission is entering a new phase, giving Iran the chance to pursue a “good and wise” peace deal, while emphasising the strength of the U.S. naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, soon to be reinforced by a second aircraft carrier.

articles.title: Shade nets offer drivers respite from heat in Karaikal

articles.description: A lower ratio can indicate untapped investment potential, especially in frontier markets where economic growth may not yet be fully reflected in stock valuations.

articles.title: UN peacekeeper dies of wounds in Lebanon
articles.description: The UN mission in Lebanon said Friday that an Indonesian peacekeeper has died from wounds sustained in a March 29 attack on his base. Arriving at an EU summit in Cyprus, French President Emmanuel Macron urged the European Union to “intensify its efforts” to help restore stability in the Middle East. France24 correspondent in Beirut, Renée Davis, reports the latest.

articles.title: Infertility Is Rising Globally: Key Factors That Are Driving This Trend
articles.description: Modern lifestyle choices also play a pivotal role in affecting fertility rates. Increasingly sedentary lifestyles, higher rates of obesity, and poor dietary habits can all contribute to infertility.

articles.title: India growth expected to be around 6.5% this year: Gita Gopinath
articles.description: India's economy is projected to grow at 6.5% this year. Global risks persist, impacting supply chains and potentially leading to food price increases. Companies are absorbing costs, but this may not last. Central banks might delay rate cuts due to muted global demand. Any oil shock will affect many sectors.

articles.title: Probe ordered after Coast Guard chopper seen flying low over Sabarimala temple: Police
articles.description: Indian Coast Guard officials said the chopper, which was on a routine training sortie from Kochi, ended up flying over the temple due to “high clouds and deteriorated weather” in area

articles.description: The French luxury house brings its travelling retail concept to Raffles City Shopping Centre, featuring signature green interiors, leather goods, a cafe kiosk and a photo booth.

articles.description: The case has quickly drawn global attention, not just for the scale of the alleged misconduct but for what it reveals about the vulnerabilities emerging at the intersection of national security and decentralised finance.

articles.title: Karnataka Heatwave Alert: Health Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao Appeals for ‘Self-Lockdown’ Amid Rising Sunstroke Cases
articles.description: Karnataka is facing a severe heatwave with temperatures crossing 42°C in several districts. Health Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao has urged citizens to follow a ‘self-lockdown’ during peak hours as sunstroke cases rise and hospitals remain on high alert.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Audrey Hepburn: "Success is like reaching an important..."
articles.description: Audrey Hepburn’s quote reveals that success doesn’t change who we are inside. True fulfilment comes from self-growth, values, and perspective, not fame or achievements.

articles.title: Ankur Warikoo's wife walked away from Rs 20 LPA job and instead chose happiness, organic farming and Rs 20,000 salary. Why?
articles.description: Ruchi Warikoo's career journey challenges traditional success metrics. She left a Rs 20 lakh salary at a Channel for organic farming, accepting an 88% pay cut. This shift brought her happiness and fulfilment. Now, she manages her husband, Ankur Warikoo's, business. Her story highlights that career paths are unpredictable, shaped by curiosity and evolving priorities, not just fixed plans.

articles.title: Delhi IRS officer’s daughter rape-murder case: Police to conduct psychological analysis of accused
articles.description: When questioned about his presence in the house, Meena allegedly lied, claiming the victim's mother had invited him in. The situation deteriorated quickly when he demanded cash. The victim refused. An argument broke out, and as she began screaming for help, Meena allegedly lost control, striking her repeatedly in the face with a lamp, leaving her with severe bruising and head injuries.
